# Test showing that standard callgraph does NOT stitch test scripts to programs

# Create a simple test that calls mcpdiff
exec mcpdiff --help
stderr 'Usage'

# Try to use standard callgraph to find connections
# This will fail because standard callgraph can't connect scripts to programs

# First, let's create a simple Go test that uses exec.Command
mkdir -p test_project
setstdin package main_test

import (
    "os/exec"
    "testing"
)

func TestCallsMCPDiff(t *testing.T) {
    cmd := exec.Command("mcpdiff", "--version")
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        t.Fatal(err)
    }
}
exec cat > test_project/test_example.go

# If we ran standard callgraph on this, it would show:
# - The test calls exec.Command
# - But NOT what specific binary (mcpdiff) is executed
# - And NOT the connection to mcpdiff's main() function

# This is the gap our testcallgraph tool will fix
